metadata.description: Standards bypass configuration for this branch

bypass.reason: Pure Python bootstrap — no branch imports by design
Pure Python bootstrap — cannot import prax (circular: prax depends on cli). _detect_aipass_home() catches detection failures silently by design.
Same silent catch as silent_catch standard — already bypassed (pure Python, no prax import allowed).
Pure Python bootstrap — cannot import prax system_logger (circular: prax depends on cli). Uses stdlib logging as fallback.
python3 reference is inside a shell command string assigned to _local_prompt_cmd — not help text, it is the cross-platform hook command itself.
print() reference is inside a shell command string assigned to _local_prompt_cmd — not a Python print() call, it is part of the inline python3 -c hook command.
Test file lives in tests/ by pytest convention — not subject to the 3-layer apps/ structure rule.
Tests must import handlers directly to unit-test private functions (_sanitize_name, init_project). Using a module entry point would prevent unit testing.
python3 reference is inside a pytest assertion string describing expected hook behavior — not help text, it is a test assertion message.
Test files use pytest docstring convention instead of AIPass META block headers.
Repo-root cross-platform installer — not a branch module, intentionally lives outside apps/ structure.
All print() calls are intentional CLI installer output, not debug prints.
main() is documented via the module-level docstring; installer entry points follow script convention.
except OSError: pass in step_secrets() is documented best-effort chmod on non-POSIX filesystems (Windows).
python3 reference on line 59 is a user-facing PATH instruction string printed to the terminal, not internal help text.
Circular import — display.py cannot import prax (prax depends on cli). Silent catches are ImportError guard for optional trigger and __main__ error handler.
Dependency-free by design — json_handler cannot import prax (circular: json_handler → prax → cli.display → json_handler). Catch regenerates corrupted JSON silently.
Python dunder convention — __all__ controls public API exports, not a constant to uppercase
Python dunder convention — __all__ controls public API exports, not a constant to uppercase
get_help() and get_introspective() are external API — called by drone, not from within CLI branch
Drone adapter cannot import prax (circular: drone_adapter → cli.main → prax → cli). Catches return graceful fallbacks.
Testing flags same silent catch as silent_catch standard — already bypassed (circular import prevents prax logger)
Testing flags same silent catch as silent_catch standard — already bypassed (dependency-free by design)
Cannot import prax — circular import (prax depends on cli). Documented in code comment.
Cannot import prax — circular import (prax depends on cli). Documented in code comment.
Prax branch detection bug — CLI logs land as unknown_branch. Prax confirmed known limitation (2026-03-23), fix pending on prax side.
cli_entry() is the console_scripts entry point — called by pyproject.toml [project.scripts] aipass = 'aipass.cli:cli_entry', not from within the codebase

notes.usage: Add entries to 'bypass' list to exclude specific violations

example.reason: Circular dependency - logger cannot import CLI

fields.standard: Standard name: cli, imports, naming, etc. (required)
fields.lines: Optional - specific line numbers to bypass
